question: Can RMB 100B over 3 years generate positive ROI, and what are the unit economics of self-operated brand building?

findings.claim: PDD's ROIC is 70-78%, among the highest of any large-cap company globally, reflecting its asset-light marketplace model
findings.data: Annualized ROIC for Q2 2025: 78.08%. TTM ROIC: 70.85%. WACC: 10.54%. PDD generates 7x its cost of capital in returns. This extraordinary ROIC is a function of the asset-light marketplace model — self-operated brands would structurally compress ROIC by increasing invested capital.

findings.claim: PDD's valuation multiples are compressed: P/E 10.5x, EV/EBITDA 6.6x, EV/FCF 5.4x — the market is already pricing in execution risk on new investments
findings.data: Trailing PE: 10.53; Forward PE: 8.44; EV/EBITDA: 6.63; EV/FCF: 5.37. Market cap fell below $150B from November 2025 peak, down 30%+. The market assigns PDD a deep value multiple despite 70%+ ROIC, suggesting skepticism about capital allocation decisions.

findings.claim: Colin Huang retains ~31% of shares outstanding with super-voting Class B shares, giving him outsized control over capital allocation
findings.data: Founder Colin Huang holds approximately 31% of total shares outstanding (including Class B super-voting shares with 10 votes per share). Economic ownership estimated at 17-28% depending on methodology. Tencent is second largest at 14%. Insider ownership total: 4.53%. Institutional ownership: ~11%.
